```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Freight Rail Car Utilization Collapse Signals Logistics Downturn",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "Association of American Railroads weekly freight car availability and utilization through html_table adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Railroad freight car utilization drops sharply when industrial demand weakens, signaling recession pressure on logistics and manufacturing. Weekly AAR reports are consistent and predictable.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Low freight utilization precedes industrial slowdown and manufacturing contraction, directly impacting industrial equipment and transportation stocks."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly freight car utilization falls more than 8% below its 12-week moving average, enter short.",
      "exit": "Exit after 10 trading days or when utilization rebounds to within 3% of 12-week MA.",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Freight utilization swings 8%+ several times per quarter in normal economic cycles, making it a frequent signal generator."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Commercial Real Estate Vacancy Spike Bearish Signal",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "CoStar or CBRE weekly commercial vacancy rate data through html_table adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Weekly CRE vacancy announcements and market reports often spike when major tenant departures or defaults occur, signaling real estate distress and reducing property valuations.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLRE",
      "rationale": "Rising CRE vacancy directly compresses real estate fundamentals, cap rates, and investor returns in the real estate sector."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If reported CRE vacancy rate rises 0.5% or more week-over-week in any major metro, short XLRE.",
      "exit": "Exit after 12 trading days or when weekly vacancy stabilizes.",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "CRE vacancies fluctuate weekly and 0.5%+ moves occur multiple times per quarter as tenants relocate or default."
  },
  {
    "idea": "College Application Volume Collapse Signals Education Demand Crisis",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "Common App weekly application submission totals through html_table adapter from public reports",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Dramatic drops in college application volumes during peak application season signal demographic or affordability collapse, reducing future revenue for education services and related consumer spending.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Lower application volumes signal weakening middle-class household formation, future discretionary spending, and consumer confidence in education ROI."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly Common App submissions fall 15% or more below the prior-year same week, short XLY.",
      "exit": "Exit after 8 trading days or when submissions recover to prior-year levels.",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Common App data is released weekly during application season (Sept–Jan) and 15%+ year-over-year swings happen several times per cycle."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Trucking Load Board Capacity Surge Signals Freight Oversupply",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "Truckstop.com and Uber Freight public load board fill-rate indices through html_table or api adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When available loads vastly outnumber available trucks on public load boards, trucking capacity crashes and freight rates collapse, signaling logistics sector weakness.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Trucking oversupply indicates weak shipping demand, depresses carrier margins, and signals industrial slowdown."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily load board available-loads-to-trucks ratio rises above 2.0 for 3 consecutive days, short XLI.",
      "exit": "Exit after 7 trading days or when ratio falls below 1.5.",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Load board ratios spike and crash weekly based on seasonal and cyclical freight demand, firing multiple times per month."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Temporary Staffing Agency Hiring Collapse Signals Labor Market Deterioration",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "ManpowerGroup and Kforce weekly hiring intention indices from earnings call transcripts and press releases through rss_count adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Weekly announcements of collapsed temporary hiring intentions from major staffing agencies precede broader labor market deterioration and consumer spending weakness.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Temp hiring collapse signals companies expect weak demand, reducing consumer spending power and leading to discretionary spending cuts."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly temp hiring intention index falls 12% or more below 3-month MA, short XLY.",
      "exit": "Exit after 10 trading days or when index stabilizes above MA.",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Temp hiring swings 12%+ quarterly and reports are published weekly, creating 3–5 signals per quarter."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Mortgage Application Volume Waterfall Signals Housing Demand Collapse",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Mortgage Bankers Association weekly applications index through html_table adapter",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sharp week-over-week drops in mortgage applications signal housing demand evaporates due to rate shocks or affordability crisis, impacting construction, materials, and consumer sentiment.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLB",
      "rationale": "Mortgage application collapses reduce demand for construction materials, lumber, and finished housing inventory."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly mortgage applications fall 20% or more below prior week, short XLB for 10 days.",
      "exit": "Exit after 10 trading days or when applications stabilize.",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Mortgage applications swing 20%+ weekly due to rate announcements and seasonal factors, firing 2–4 times per month."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Natural Gas Storage Injection Collapse Signals Energy Demand Shock",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "EIA weekly natural gas storage updates through fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When weekly natural gas storage injections plummet (or turn to unexpected draws), it signals either demand spike or supply shock, creating energy price volatility and sector stress.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Unexpected storage draws or injection collapses support energy prices and create near-term energy sector gains from price recovery."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly EIA gas storage injection falls below historical 5-year minimum for that week by 50% or more, go long XLE.",
      "exit": "Exit after 8 trading days or when injection rebounds to normal levels.",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Gas storage swings 50%+ vs. seasonal norms occur 4–8 times per year, particularly in winter and summer transition periods."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Credit Card Processing Volume Plunge Signals Consumer Spending Halt",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Visa and Mastercard weekly transaction volume indices through html_table or rss_count adapter from earnings/investor updates",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Sharp drops in credit card transaction volumes precede broader consumer spending collapses and signal immediate consumer confidence crisis.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Spending volume collapse directly compresses revenue for retail, hospitality, and consumer discretionary companies."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly credit card transaction volume falls 15% or more below 4-week MA, short XLY.",
      "exit": "Exit after 10 trading days or when volume recovers to MA.",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Credit card volumes experience 15%+ swings quarterly due to seasonal patterns and economic shocks, creating 2–4 monthly signals."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Copper Futures Volatility Spike Signals Risk-Off Macro Repricing",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "Copper futures daily prices and 20-day volatility through price_only adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Copper volatility spikes often precede broad industrial and emerging market stress, as copper is a leading macroeconomic risk proxy due to its use in construction and manufacturing.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "High copper volatility signals macro uncertainty, reducing industrial capex and equipment demand."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 20-day copper futures volatility rises 40% or more above its 60-day MA, short XLI.",
      "exit": "Exit after 7 trading days or when copper volatility mean-reverts below MA + 20%.",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Copper volatility swings 40%+ on macro headlines and Fed announcements 2–4 times per month."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Regional Bank CDS Spread Widening Signals Financial System Stress",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED regional bank CDS indices (5-year spreads) and daily price tracking through fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When regional bank credit default swap spreads widen sharply, it signals market-perceived stress in the mid-sized financial system, often preceding credit market seizures or deposit runs.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "CDS spread widening indicates markets price higher default risk for regional banks, compressing equity valuations and reducing lending."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily regional bank CDS spread widens 30+ basis points in a single day or rises 50+ bps over 5 days, short XLF.",
      "exit": "Exit after 8 trading days or when spreads contract 20+ bps.",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Regional bank CDS spreads experience 30+ bps daily moves during periods of financial uncertainty, which occur multiple times per quarter."
  }
]
```
